title: Campaigns OS Run Record v0
description: A per-run manifest for one Campaigns OS run. Keyed by one canonical run_id. References source artifacts by {path, schema_version, sha256} and carries normalized observation arrays — it does NOT re-embed full artifact bodies (so it survives upstream schema drift). Capture is always local; consent gates remit only. See docs/workflow-findings-sidecar.md (Run Telemetry).

run_id.description: The single canonical run identity, minted at the run boundary. Also the idempotency key for remit — the endpoint upserts on run_id so retries/reruns never double-count.

package_version.description: Version of the @nextcommerce/campaigns-os package that produced this record.

command.description: The command that ran, e.g. "run-record", "start", "qa run".

argv_shape.description: The flag NAMES present on the command line (e.g. "--packet", "--write"), never their raw values. Shape only — minimization.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the record was assembled.

consent_state.description: Resolved telemetry consent at capture time. Gates remit only; capture is always local.

consent_source.description: Where consent_state was resolved from. "default" means unknown/non-interactive — fails closed to off.

remit_attempted.description: Whether a remit POST was attempted for this run.

remit_ok.description: Whether the remit POST succeeded. null when no attempt was made.

remit_error.description: Error message from a failed remit, so a dropped send is visible and not silent. null on success or no attempt.

remit_endpoint.description: Endpoint path the run was remitted to, e.g. "/api/runs". null when no attempt was made.

identity.description: Best-effort run identity — the join keys that make telemetry useful. Missing identity never blocks capture.

entry_point_shape.description: How the run was entered, e.g. "packet", "spec", "map-id". Shape, not a raw path or id.

artifacts.description: References to this run's source artifacts. References, not copies — this is what survives upstream schema drift. schema_version/sha256 are nullable for artifacts that are not on disk or whose hash could not be read.

path.description: Relativized-or-hashed path. Never an absolute contributor filesystem path.

observations.description: Normalized observation arrays — the extracted structural signal. Codes/IDs/dispositions only, never raw artifact bodies.

spec_validation_rule_ids.description: ruleId of every spec.validation violation that fired.

adapter_decisions.description: Normalized adapter decision values (strategy enums), not source files.

gap_classes.description: Distinct assertion families that produced a QA exception (e.g. "funnel-flow", "meta-tags").

finding_ids.description: IDs of this run's Workflow Findings (the per-run findings snapshot, by reference). The journal is the single writer; this is an exact snapshot when findings carry run_id.

findings_journal.description: Integrity metadata for the findings journal snapshot. Present when malformed JSONL lines were skipped so the snapshot is visibly incomplete.

surfaces.description: Improvement surfaces this run's signal should inform. A list, not an enum — real signal is rarely one surface.

surface_confidence.description: Optional free-form confidence hint for primary_surface, e.g. "low".
